engineGuide.h2: Aston Martin Virage Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Aston Martin Virage across its 2011-2012 production run, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK Aston Martin specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 5.9L Petrol
entries.history: The sole V12 powerplant for the ultra-rare Virage, calibrated for 490bhp comfort-performance balance between DB9 and DBS.
compatibleVariants: Virage Coupe, Virage Volante, Virage Carbon Edition, Virage Launch Specification
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - documented failure point on VH-platform V12s between 40k-70k miles requiring urgent attention.
Ignition coil pack degradation - individual coil failure causes misfires, rough idle and EML illumination on high-mileage units.
Cam cover gasket oil seepage - common leakage requiring engine-out labour for proper rectification on bonded-aluminium chassis.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AM25 Aston Martin Virage engine replacement

entries.title: 5.9L Petrol
entries.history: Late-production revision of the AM25 with minor calibration refinements for final 2012 Virage units before model discontinuation.
compatibleVariants: Virage Coupe (late production), Virage Volante (late production)
commonFailures: Water pump bearing wear - coolant circulation failure can cause overheating and head gasket compromise on VH-platform V12s.
Valve stem seal degradation - oil consumption increases beyond 50k miles, leading to blue smoke and catalytic converter risk.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AM25/1 Aston Martin Virage engine replacement

entries.title: 5.9L Petrol
entries.history: Displacement-specific designation for the 5935cc naturally aspirated V12, used for technical documentation and specialist rebuild reference.
compatibleVariants: Virage Coupe, Virage Volante, all UK-market Virage variants with standard calibration
commonFailures: Rear main seal oil leakage - high-labour rectification requiring engine removal on bonded-aluminium VH chassis vehicles.
Carbon buildup on intake components - deposit accumulation affecting idle quality and throttle response on high-mileage units.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AM25-5935 Aston Martin Virage engine replacement

engineGuide.closing: Can't find your engine code? Enter your registration number above - we'll instantly identify the exact unit, its technical specifications, and live replacement prices from UK Aston Martin Virage engine specialists. Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Aston Martin Virage Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Aston Martin Virage's AM25 5.9L V12 is a refined but complex powerplant; timing chain tensioner wear, ignition coil failure and oil leakage are the most prominent issues driving replacement enquiries for this ultra-rare grand tourer. Every rebuilt AM25 engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Aston Martin specialists.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why VH-Platform V12s Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Virage Coupe, Virage Volante (2011-2012, AM25 & AM25/1)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner wear allows chain slack, causing rattling noise and potential valve timing disruption on the VH-platform V12 architecture.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioners, guides and chain before catastrophic failure; requires front engine access
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before chain jump; extends engine life 30k+ miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of specialist-rebuilt AM25 V12 with upgraded tensioners, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Definitive solution; restores factory performance with enhanced reliability for collector-grade ownership

problems.recommendation: On a Virage valued at £53k-£65k, preventative tensioner replacement at 40k miles offers strong value if the engine is otherwise healthy. If rattling is already present or mileage exceeds 60k, a rebuilt AM25 with upgraded components is the safer long-term investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt AM25 engine prices for your Aston Martin Virage

problems.h4: Ignition Coil Pack Failure - Misfire and Rough Running on AM25 V12
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Virage Coupe, Virage Volante (2011-2012, AM25)
problems.rootCause: Individual coil packs degrade with heat cycling and vibration, causing intermittent misfires, rough idle and EML illumination on the 12-cylinder layout.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all 12 ignition coils with OEM-spec units; diagnostic reset and road test included
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses misfire symptoms; recommended at 50k miles as preventative maintenance on high-value V12

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of specialist-rebuilt AM25 V12 with upgraded ignition components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Preferred if misfires have caused catalytic damage or if engine has other high-mileage wear indicators

problems.recommendation: For isolated misfire codes with otherwise healthy compression and oil consumption, full coil replacement at a VH-platform specialist offers excellent value. If multiple systems show wear, consider the rebuilt engine route for comprehensive peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get quotes for AM25 ignition system repair or replacement for your Virage

problems.group: Oil Leak from Cam Cover or Rear Main Seal
problems.h4: Oil Leak from Cam Cover or Rear Main Seal - Labour-Intensive Rectification
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Virage Coupe, Virage Volante (2011-2012, AM25 & AM25/1)
problems.rootCause: Gasket material degradation combined with VH-platform engine mounting geometry creates seepage points at cam covers and rear main seal interface.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove engine, replace cam cover gaskets and/or rear main seal with updated materials; reassembly and fluid refill
repairOptions.longevity: Effective repair if engine internals are sound; labour cost dominates due to VH chassis access constraints

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of specialist-rebuilt AM25 V12 with all seals updated, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Optimal if leak coincides with other wear indicators; eliminates future seal concerns on collector vehicle

problems.recommendation: Given the engine-out labour required for seal replacement on the VH platform, owners of high-mileage Virage examples often find the rebuilt engine route more cost-effective long-term, especially when preserving matching-numbers provenance isn't the priority.
problems.cta: Compare AM25 engine replacement costs for your Aston Martin Virage

problems.h4: Excessive Oil Consumption - Valve Seal or Piston Ring Wear on High-Mileage AM25
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Virage Coupe, Virage Volante (2011-2012, AM25)
problems.rootCause: Valve stem seal hardening and/or piston ring wear allows oil into combustion chambers, causing blue smoke, catalytic converter risk and frequent top-ups.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace valve stem seals, reassemble with updated gaskets; compression test post-repair
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if consumption is moderate and compression remains healthy; does not address ring wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of specialist-rebuilt AM25 V12 with upgraded seals and rings, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for consumption exceeding 1L/1,000 miles or if compression testing indicates ring wear

problems.recommendation: For Virage owners planning long-term retention, a rebuilt AM25 with upgraded internal components offers superior reliability versus piecemeal seal replacement, particularly given the labour intensity of VH-platform engine work.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt AM25 engines with upgraded seals for your Aston Martin Virage

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Aston Martin Virage
finalCta.paragraph: The AM25 V12 is the heart of your ultra-rare grand tourer, and failure doesn't have to mean the end of the road. EngineMarket connects you directly with UK Aston Martin specialists who understand VH-platform intricacies, carbon ceramic brake integration, and the importance of matching-numbers provenance for collector-grade vehicles. Compare rebuilt, reconditioned and used AM25 engine prices from vetted suppliers, all backed by minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranties and nationwide delivery to your chosen specialist workshop.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Aston Martin Virage Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Aston Martin Virage and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

fuelTypes.h2: Aston Martin Virage Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Aston Martin Virage engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from the sole 5.9L naturally aspirated V12 offered across all variants.

items.title: Aston Martin Virage Petrol Engines
items.description: The Aston Martin Virage was offered exclusively with a 5.9-litre naturally aspirated V12 petrol engine (AM25), calibrated for 490bhp to deliver refined grand touring performance between the DB9 and DBS.
items.descriptor: The Aston Martin Virage was offered exclusively with a 5.9-litre naturally aspirated V12 petrol engine (AM25), calibrated for 490bhp to deliver refined grand touring performance between the DB9 and DBS.
items.familiesLabel: Common Aston Martin Virage Petrol Engine Families
families: AM25 - sole V12 code for all Virage variants, timing chain tensioner and coil pack failure points documented
AM25/1 - late-production revision with minor calibration refinements for final 2012 units
AM25-5935 - displacement-specific designation used for technical documentation and specialist rebuild reference
knownFor: Refined, torque-rich V12 character with 490bhp output distinguishing Virage from DB9 (470bhp) and DBS (510bhp)
VH-platform timing chain tensioner wear between 40k-70k miles representing the most significant failure risk
High labour costs for rectification due to bonded-aluminium chassis access constraints on VH architecture
Strong collector appeal driving demand for matching-numbers, specialist-rebuilt AM25 replacement engines

engineYears.h2: Aston Martin Virage Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine specifications across the Aston Martin Virage's 2011-2012 production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their AM25 V12 and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Aston Martin Virage was produced for just 18 months (March 2011 to September 2012), with the AM25 5.9L V12 fitted unchanged across all variants throughout its brief production life.
keyChanges: March 2011: Virage Coupe launched with AM25 V12, 490bhp, Touchtronic 2 transmission
September 2011: Virage Volante convertible variant introduced, same AM25 powertrain
2012: Carbon Edition and Launch Specification limited variants released with identical engine calibration
September 2012: Virage discontinued; AM25 V12 production ceased for this model line
mainEngines: AM25 - 5.9L petrol V12 (Virage Coupe / Volante / Carbon Edition / Launch Specification)
AM25/1 - 5.9L petrol V12 (late-production 2012 variants with minor calibration refinements)
knownFor: Ultra-rare production volume (~1,000 units globally; ~10 typically available in UK market)
AM25 V12 calibrated for 490bhp, positioning Virage between DB9 (470bhp) and DBS (510bhp)
VH-platform timing chain tensioner wear representing the most significant failure risk for replacement enquiries
Strong collector appeal driving demand for matching-numbers, specialist-rebuilt AM25 engines with documented provenance
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2012 Aston Martin Virage replacement engines

faq.h2: Aston Martin Virage Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does an Aston Martin Virage engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A rebuilt AM25 V12 engine for the Aston Martin Virage typically costs £15,000-£25,000 supply-only in the UK, with supply & fit adding £3,500-£7,500 depending on workshop location and vehicle accessibility.
keyPoints: Rebuilt AM25 units include upgraded timing components and 12-month warranty
Used engines are extremely rare due to ~1,000 Virage units produced globally
Specialist VH-platform labour rates reflect the complexity of bonded-aluminium chassis access
Matching-numbers provenance commands premium pricing for collector-grade vehicles
items.cta: Compare rebuilt AM25 engine prices for your Aston Martin Virage

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in an Aston Martin Virage?
items.answer: Yes, given Virage market values of £53k-£65k, a £15k-£25k rebuilt AM25 engine often represents better value than parting out or scrapping, especially for collector-grade examples with desirable specifications.
keyPoints: Virage values remain strong due to ultra-rare 18-month production window
Rebuilt AM25 engines restore performance with enhanced reliability via upgraded components
Matching-numbers documentation preserves provenance for future resale value
Specialist supply & fit ensures correct VH-platform integration and commissioning
items.cta: Find rebuilt Aston Martin Virage engines with warranty coverage

items.question: What causes timing chain failure on the Virage AM25 V12?
items.answer: Hydraulic tensioner wear on the VH-platform V12 allows chain slack, causing rattling and potential valve timing disruption; failure typically occurs between 40,000-70,000 miles if not addressed preventatively.
keyPoints: Tensioner failure is the most documented catastrophic risk for AM25 engines
Preventative replacement at 40k miles offers strong value versus post-failure rebuild costs
Upgraded tensioners and guides are fitted as standard on specialist-rebuilt AM25 units
Early warning signs include cold-start rattling and EML illumination with timing-related codes
items.cta: Compare AM25 timing chain repair versus replacement costs for your Virage

items.question: Do you supply matching-numbers AM25 engines for the Aston Martin Virage?
items.answer: Yes, our network includes specialists who source and rebuild AM25 engines with matching-numbers documentation for collector-grade Virage restoration projects requiring provenance verification.
keyPoints: Matching-numbers engines command premium pricing due to provenance value
Documentation includes build sheets, dyno certificates and calibration verification
Specialists verify engine code, calibration and VIN correlation prior to dispatch
Preferred option for concours restoration and investment-grade Virage ownership
items.cta: Enquire about matching-numbers AM25 engines for your Aston Martin Virage

items.question: What is the difference between Virage Coupe and Volante engines?
items.answer: The AM25 V12 engine is identical between Virage Coupe and Volante variants; calibration, power output and failure patterns are consistent across both body styles produced 2011-2012.
keyPoints: Both variants use the same AM25, AM25/1 and AM25-5935 engine codes
490bhp output and 5.9L displacement are consistent across Coupe and Volante
Replacement engine compatibility is universal; body style does not affect engine selection
Volante convertible examples may have slightly higher mileage affecting engine wear assessment
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin Virage Coupe and Volante engine replacement prices

items.question: How long does a Virage AM25 engine replacement take?
items.answer: A specialist-rebuilt AM25 engine supply & fit typically takes 5-10 working days, including removal, installation, ECU adaptation, fluid refill and post-commissioning road test validation.
keyPoints: Engine removal on VH-platform requires bonded-aluminium chassis expertise
ECU adaptation and calibration verification add 1-2 days to standard fitment time
Post-installation road test ensures proper integration with carbon ceramic brake systems
Lead times vary by specialist workshop capacity and component availability
items.cta: Find Aston Martin Virage engine supply & fit specialists in your region

items.question: Are used AM25 engines reliable for the Aston Martin Virage?
items.answer: Used AM25 engines can be reliable if sourced from verified, low-mileage donors with full service history and compression testing; however, extreme rarity limits availability and increases due diligence requirements.
keyPoints: Only ~1,000 Virage units produced globally; used engine supply is extremely constrained
Compression and leak-down testing essential prior to purchase of any used AM25 unit
Limited warranty options available versus comprehensive coverage on rebuilt units
Matching engine code and calibration verification critical for compatibility assurance
items.cta: Check used AM25 engine availability for your Aston Martin Virage

items.question: What warranty comes with a rebuilt Aston Martin Virage engine?
items.answer: Every rebuilt AM25 engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Aston Martin specialists, covering components and workmanship against manufacturing defects.
keyPoints: Warranty terms vary by specialist; confirm coverage details before purchase
Upgraded components (tensioners, seals, coils) typically included in warranty scope
Dyno testing certificates and build documentation support warranty validation
Nationwide specialist network enables warranty service access across UK regions
items.cta: Compare warranty terms for rebuilt Aston Martin Virage engines

items.question: Can I upgrade the power output of my Virage AM25 engine?
items.answer: While the AM25 V12 was calibrated for 490bhp from factory, specialist tuning options exist; however, any power increase should be balanced against reliability priorities for this ultra-rare grand tourer.
keyPoints: ECU remapping can extract modest power gains while retaining drivability
Forced induction conversions are possible but compromise original character and reliability
Upgraded cooling and lubrication recommended for any performance modification
Collector-grade Virage owners often prioritise factory-specification preservation over power increases
items.cta: Enquire about AM25 engine specification options for your Aston Martin Virage

items.question: Where is the AM25 engine code located on the Aston Martin Virage?
items.answer: The AM25 engine code is stamped on the engine block near the cylinder head interface; verification against your VIN ensures correct replacement engine compatibility for your specific Virage variant.
keyPoints: Engine code location accessible with engine bay inspection; specialist assistance recommended
Calibration variant (AM25 vs AM25/1) may require ECU diagnostic verification
Matching engine code critical for maintaining provenance on collector-grade examples
DVLA registration lookup instantly confirms engine specification for your Virage
